note: AUD5 fix for finding F-09 (docs/novakai/audit/04-findings.md). SCOPE: .mjs TOOLING — real verification is node --test tools/novakai/onboard.test.mjs (the F-09 assertion failed pre-fix: onboard printed no handoff-freshness state).

intent.problem: attack A8: the Stop-hook freshness nudge fires only on a clean session Stop — a session that crashes mid-operation (two zero-byte .git/index.lock artifacts prove it happens here) never warns anyone, so the next session starts on a stale handoff silently.
intent.approach: onboard.mjs STEP 7 runs handoff-fresh --check at session START (the SessionStart hook already runs onboard, so the surface is forced): fresh prints ✓ HANDOFF FRESH; stale prints ⚠ HANDOFF LAGS THE CODE + treat-as-suspect instructions. A nudge, not a gate — onboard's exit code stays about map trust; F4 in CI remains the hard backstop.
intent.rationale: start-of-session is crash-proof: whatever killed the last session, the next one always onboards. onboard.test.mjs (new, in the suite) locks the surface.
